audit_items.proof_trace: LinkerInputBinary::data is freed exactly once in the destructor body (delete[] data, L113) and all accesses to binary->data occur during perform_link() while ctx->inputs holds all unique_ptr<LinkerInputBinary> alive. Section::data.data_segments is freed at most once in ~Section() (guarded by section_code==Data check, L91-93); ~DataSegment() is trivially generated and never dereferences the DataSegment::data alias into binary->data. All other raw pointer aliases (StringSlice::start, FunctionImport::foreign_binary, Section::binary) have strictly shorter lifetimes than the data they point to, verified across all call sites.
